# Nav0

> A minimal, privacy-focused web browser. Zero telemetry, no accounts, no AI gimmicks — just clean, fast browsing. Free and open source under the MIT License, built on Electron and Chromium for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Nav0 collects no data, makes no idle network requests, and stores everything locally on your device. Its guiding principle is "Browse. Nothing more." The links below are the canonical entry points to the project. For the full text of the most important pages in one file, see https://nav0.org/llms-full.txt.

## Start here

- [Nav0 — Browse. Nothing more.](https://nav0.org/): What Nav0 is, who it is for, and how a zero-data browser compares to the mainstream.
- [Download Nav0](https://nav0.org/install): Installers for Windows, macOS (Intel and Apple Silicon), and Linux, plus build-from-source instructions.
- [Frequently Asked Questions](https://nav0.org/faq): Direct answers on data collection, accounts, extensions, ad blocking, and how Nav0 differs from Chrome, Brave, and Firefox.
- [Philosophy](https://nav0.org/guide/philosophy): Why Nav0 does one thing — browse the web — and deliberately omits AI, sync, crypto, and VPN features.
- [Getting Started](https://nav0.org/guide/getting-started): Install Nav0, set it as default, and learn the core workflow in a few minutes.

## Browser comparisons

- [Nav0 vs Brave](https://nav0.org/blog/nav0-vs-brave): Why real privacy shouldn't ship with a crypto wallet, an ad network, and a VPN upsell.
- [Nav0 vs Firefox](https://nav0.org/blog/nav0-vs-firefox): Keeping Firefox's ideals while removing default telemetry, account nudges, and sponsored content.
- [Nav0 vs Safari](https://nav0.org/blog/nav0-vs-safari): Privacy by default versus privacy on Apple's terms, tied to iCloud and one ecosystem.
- [Nav0 vs Microsoft Edge](https://nav0.org/blog/nav0-vs-edge): The browser that reports to Redmond — telemetry, Bing, Copilot, and shopping tools nobody asked for.
- [Nav0 vs Arc](https://nav0.org/blog/nav0-vs-arc): Maximalism versus minimalism — adding UI and AI versus stripping the browser down.

## Privacy essays

- [Your Browser Is Watching You](https://nav0.org/blog/your-browser-is-watching-you): Exactly what modern browsers track, why it matters, and what you can do about it.
- [Incognito Mode Is Not Private](https://nav0.org/blog/incognito-mode-is-not-private): What private-browsing modes actually hide — and who can still see you.
- [Cookie Banners Are Privacy Theater](https://nav0.org/blog/cookie-banners-are-privacy-theater): How consent banners became a tax on attention while tracking quietly continued.

## Performance benchmarks

- [Nav0 vs Chrome: Performance Benchmark](https://nav0.org/blog/nav0-vs-chrome-performance-benchmark): On a MacBook Pro M1, Nav0 used 48.5% less memory and 86.3% less CPU than Chrome across 10–50 tabs.
- [Nav0 vs Chrome: Data Consumption](https://nav0.org/blog/nav0-vs-chrome-data-consumption): Measured byte for byte across 15 sites — Nav0 transferred 17.7% less data and made 29.1% fewer requests.

## More

- [Blog](https://nav0.org/blog/): Essays on privacy, browser comparisons, performance, and the open web.
- [Release Notes](https://nav0.org/releases/): Every shipped version, with literal patch notes and no marketing-speak.
- [Features](https://nav0.org/guide/features): The full, deliberately short list of what Nav0 does.
- [What Nav0 Doesn't Do](https://nav0.org/guide/un-features): The "un-features" — AI, sync, crypto, VPN, accounts, telemetry — and why they are absent by design.
- [Privacy](https://nav0.org/guide/privacy): How privacy is the architecture, not a setting you have to find and toggle.
